assertions.issues: Curve library router.getBestRouteAndOutput() throws 'This method exists only for L2 networks' for all tested token pairs on Base (chainId 8453). Despite Base being an L2, the Curve library appears to reject the router method. Likely a @curvefi/api library bug or misconfiguration for Base chain support.

evidence.network: All 6 non-Ethereum chains checked: none returned 'Curve only supports Ethereum' in curve_error. Errors are route-specific or absent.

frictions.description: BSC default token pair (USDT→WBNB) has no Curve route. Had to discover a working pair (USDC→USDT) by trying stablecoin combinations. Not documented which pairs are Curve-supported per chain.
frictions.resolution: Tried USDC→USDT stablecoin pair which succeeded on BSC.

frictions.description: Spandex returned 'No providers returned a successful quote' for all test requests, so comparison testing (Spandex vs Curve) was not possible — only Curve results could be validated.
frictions.resolution: Assertions only require Curve quote verification, not Spandex. Spandex failures are documented as a known issue (rate limits).

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 6 passed, 2 failed. VAL-CURVE-002 (Base) and VAL-CURVE-004 (Optimism) fail — Curve library returns errors for all tested token pairs on these chains ('This method exists only for L2 networks' / 'You must call getBestRouteAndOutput first'). Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, Avalanche work correctly. BSC works with stablecoin pairs (not default USDT→WBNB). VAL-CURVE-008 passes — no 'Curve only supports Ethereum' messages appear anywhere.
